lede: A look at September 13 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Yeonjun and Tom Learoyd-Lahrs, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Taliban
events.desc: Taliban insurgents attack the United States consulate in Herat, Afghanistan, with two members of the Afghan National Police reported dead and about 20 civilians injured.

events.title: Hurricane Gilbert
events.desc: Hurricane Gilbert is the strongest recorded hurricane in the Western Hemisphere, later replaced by Hurricane Wilma in 2005 (based on barometric pressure).

events.title: Super Mario Bros.
events.desc: Super Mario Bros. is released in Japan for the NES, which starts the Super Mario series of platforming games.

events.title: Spantax Flight 995
events.desc: Spantax Flight 995 crashes at Málaga Airport during a rejected takeoff, killing 50 of the 394 people on board.

events.title: Nikita Khrushchev
events.desc: Nikita Khrushchev is appointed General Secretary of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union.

events.title: Halford Mackinder
events.desc: Mackinder, Ollier and Brocherel make the first ascent of Batian (5,199 m – 17,058 ft), the highest peak of Mount Kenya.

events.title: Phineas Gage
events.desc: Vermont railroad worker Phineas Gage survives an iron rod 1+1⁄4 inches (3.2 cm) in diameter being driven through his brain; the reported effects on his behavior and personality stimulate discussion of the nature of the brain and its functions.

events.title: War of 1812
events.desc: War of 1812: A supply wagon sent to relieve Fort Harrison is ambushed in the Attack at the Narrows.

events.title: Louis XVI
events.desc: King Louis XVI accepts the new French constitution.

events.title: Temple of Jupiter Optimus Maximus
events.desc: The Temple of Jupiter Optimus Maximus on Rome's Capitoline Hill is dedicated on the ides of September.

births.name: Yeonjun
births.line: South Korean singer, songwriter and dancer

births.name: Tavis Smiley
births.line: American talk show host, journalist, and author

births.name: Guntis Ulmanis
births.line: Latvian economist and politician, 5th President of Latvia

deaths.text: Charles James Fox, English soldier and politician, Secretary of State for Foreign and Commonwealth Affairs

observances.text: Christian feast day: Feast of the Cross (Assyrian Church of the East)
